title: Reconciliation
description: The reconciler compares the current component tree against saved state and computes the minimal diff.
step_render_body: the component tree is evaluated, producing a fiber tree with instance nodes
step_load_state_body: the previous state is loaded from Memory
step_diff_body: the reconciler compares previous and current instance nodes by ID (path-based)
step_dependency_graph_body: builds a parent-child dependency graph from node paths
step_topological_sort_body: orders nodes so parents deploy before children
step_apply_body: handlers run for created and updated nodes, cleanup runs for deleted nodes
step_save_state_body: the new state is persisted to Memory
change_detection_intro: The reconciler detects three types of changes:
change_creates: <0>Creates:</0> nodes in the current tree whose ID wasn't in the previous state
change_deletes: <0>Deletes:</0> nodes in the previous state whose ID isn't in the current tree
change_updates: <0>Updates:</0> nodes present in both, whose props differ (deep equality check)
matching_body: Nodes are matched by their <0>id</0>, which is derived from the path (e.g., <1>app.aws.web-site-blog</1>). There is no type-based matching.
deep_equal_body: Props are compared using deep equality (<0>deepEqual</0>). Identical props, even new object references, won't trigger a handler re-run.
parallel_deployment_intro: After computing the change set, the reconciler builds a dependency graph from path hierarchy (parents must deploy before children) and groups independent nodes into parallel batches. The <0>ChangeSet</0> includes:
parallel_deployment_batches: <0>parallelBatches</0>: groups of nodes that can be deployed concurrently
source_intro: The reconciliation logic lives in <0>runtime/src/reconcile.ts</0>. Key exports:
source_build_dependency_graph: <0>buildDependencyGraph()</0>: builds parent-child dependency graph from node paths
source_compute_parallel_batches: <0>computeParallelBatches()</0>: groups independent nodes for concurrent deployment
